name: Pico San Millán - Enrique del Ribero Loop
name_original: Pico Millán y Camino de las Cascadas — circular por el Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda
description: This difficult 9.1-mile jogging loop to Pico San Millán offers challenging climbs, diverse forests, and panoramic views in Sierra de la Dema
region_intro: Jogging around Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de la Demanda in Burgos. The region is characterized by its mature beech forests, the winding Urbión River, and the challenging ascent to San Millán Peak. These natural features create a varied environment, from riverside paths to mountain trails, suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión The most popular jogging route is Pico San Millán - Enrique del Ribero Loop, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete, featuring a challenging ascent towards San Millán Peak. Another top favourite among local runners is Pradoluengo Village – La Pasada Shelter loop from Pradoluengo, a difficult 19.8 miles (31.8 km) path that traverses extensive sections of the Sierra de la Demanda. Local runners also love the Pradoluengo Village – Cruz De Guirnaldo loop from Pradoluengo, a 15.3 miles (24.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain around Pradoluengo, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Jogging around Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión is defined by its beech forests, the Urbión River valley, and the rugged Sierra de la Demanda. The network offers options primarily for experienced runners seeking challenging routes. The routes in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: There are over 25 dedicated running routes around Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión, offering a variety of experiences within the Sierra de la Demanda. Most of these routes are considered difficult, catering to experienced runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: The terrain in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión is quite varied, ranging from challenging mountain trails to paths through mature beech forests and along the Urbión River. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, especially on routes leading towards peaks like San Millán.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate running routes in the area?
A: While the majority of routes in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión are classified as difficult, there is one moderate running route available. There are currently no easy routes listed, indicating the area is primarily suited for runners seeking a challenge.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running?
A: You'll encounter stunning natural beauty, including the mature beech forests and the Urbión River. The area is also home to spectacular waterfalls, particularly along the Path of the Altuzarra Waterfalls. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán (2,131 m)&lt;/a&gt; peak or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3546522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de la Demanda Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: Yes, many of the routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050021271/pico-san-millan-enrique-del-ribero-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico San Millán - Enrique del Ribero Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 14.6 km with a significant ascent.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: The Sierra de la Demanda, where Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión is located, is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: The running routes in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the challenging ascents, the natural beauty of the beech forests, and the rewarding views from peaks like San Millán.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options available?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán loop from Pradoluengo&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive mileage, covering over 36 km. The region is also traversed by the GR 82 La Demanda Mountain Range trail, providing options for very long runs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: The region's beech forests are particularly stunning in autumn with vibrant colors, while spring offers lush greenery and pleasant temperatures. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, but higher trails provide cooler air. Winter running is possible but may require appropriate gear for snow and ice, particularly on higher peaks.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the **Path of the Altuzarra Waterfalls** is a notable route that leads through a deep ravine, revealing spectacular waterfalls like the Small Jump, Middle Jump, and Double Jump, all set amidst lush beech trees. This area is a significant draw for its unique ecological beauty.

Q: What are some of the highest points I can reach on a run?
A: The highest point in the province of Burgos, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán (2,131 m)&lt;/a&gt;, is accessible via challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050021271/pico-san-millan-enrique-del-ribero-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico San Millán - Enrique del Ribero Loop&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that includes a significant ascent towards this peak, offering breathtaking views.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families with children?
A: Given that most routes are classified as difficult, with significant distances and elevation gains, many may not be suitable for young children. However, shorter, flatter sections along the Urbión River or within the beech forests could offer more family-friendly options for a gentle walk or light jog, though dedicated 'easy' running routes are limited.
tips: The San Millán or Torruco peak is one of the giants of the Sierra de la Demanda, it has an altitude of 2,131 meters and is the geographical ceiling of the province of Burgos. The relative smoothness of the southern slopes facilitate the possibility of designing different trail running itineraries to the pingorota at the top, which has splendid views and is always comforting after the endless climb from Pineda de la Sierra.
Another place to take a break and to look into the distance, great views.
Spectacular is to say little, in autumn, with good flow, it has to be beastly

faqs.question: Is the Pico San Millán - Enrique del Ribero Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as difficult and is best suited for experienced joggers and trail runners. It features significant elevation changes, very steep sections, and varied terrain that demands good physical condition.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Pico San Millán - Enrique del Ribero Loop?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse mix of surfaces, including tracks, paths, and firebreaks. You should be prepared for very steep climbs, some with gradients exceeding 8%, and scree (pedreras) in the final kilometers. Depending on water flow, you may also need to carefully cross rivers and streams.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by the summit of San Millán (2,131 m), enjoy impressive views from MajadaGarrula, and encounter the beautiful Double Waterfall. The trail also traverses varied landscapes, from oak groves to beech forests and reforested pine stands.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates significant sections of the Senda Enrique del Ribero and the Senda de las Cascadas. You will also find smaller portions of the Ascensión al San Millán, Camino San Millan - Cabeza Águilez, and Puente Tres Aguas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable across seasons, but autumn is particularly recommended for the vibrant colors of the beech forests. In winter, snow and ice significantly increase the difficulty, requiring specialized equipment like crampons and ice axes. Clear days offer extensive panoramas from the summit.

faqs.question: Is the Pico San Millán - Enrique del Ribero Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, it's important to note that it passes through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Area, which is a Special Protection Area (ZEPA) for its birdlife. Always keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on challenging terrain and near river crossings.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access the Pico San Millán - Enrique del Ribero Loop. However, as it's within the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Area, please respect all natural park regulations, especially regarding waste, noise, and wildlife.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details are not provided in the available information. It is advisable to check local maps or community forums for designated parking areas near the start of the Senda Enrique Rivero or the general access points for Pico San Millán.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, appropriate jogging or trail running shoes with good grip, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A map or GPS device is also highly recommended due to the varied terrain and potential for steep sections.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this route?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for the final kilometers to the summit of Pico San Millán, which often involve scree and very steep gradients. Patience and good physical condition are key here. If attempting in winter, specialized equipment like crampons and an ice axe are essential due to snow and ice. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.
